title: Per-byte frequency table sorted by byte value
prompt: Read all bytes from standard input. Count how often each byte value (0 through 255) appears. For every byte value with a non-zero count, write one line of the form `<byte_decimal> <count>\n` to standard output. The lines must be in ascending byte-value order. Empty input produces empty output. Exit with status 0.

input_schema.stdin: <input bytes, possibly empty, up to 100000 bytes>

output_schema.stdout: <zero or more lines of '<byte_decimal> <count>\n' in ascending byte-value order>

notes_input_convention: Zero reads the input bytes from argv[1] because Zero 0.1.2 has no exposed stdin capability; the bytes are interpreted identically to stdin reads in the other languages. All other languages read all bytes from stdin until EOF. The harness adapts per language.
